Bash("/c/Program Files/GitHub CLI/gh.exe" pr create --title "Fix 6 RLS security vulnerabilities" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Security audit of Supabase RLS policies revealed 6 vulnerabilities, all fixed in migration `023_fix_rls_security_audit_2026_03_12.sql`:

- **`bed_photos` / `planting_photos`** â€” RLS was completely disabled; any unauthenticated request could read or write all rows. RLS now enabled with `authenticated` + `user_id` scoped policies.
- **`custom_tasks` / `planting_task_events`** â€” Policies used the `public` role with `qual = true`, meaning fully open to unauthenticated callers. Replaced with `authenticated` + `user_id` scoped policies.
- **`plants`** â€” `authenticated_all` policy \(qual = true\) was OR'd with the more restrictive per-command policies, rendering them all ineffective. Any authenticated user could modify or delete any plant. Policy dropped; `update_own_plants` also rebuilt with explicit `WITH CHECK`.
- **`planting_health_logs`** â€” Policy targeted `public` role instead of `authenticated`, and had no explicit `WITH CHECK`. Replaced with a correct `authenticated` policy.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify authenticated users can still read/write their own rows on all affected tables
- [ ] Verify unauthenticated requests \(anon key only\) are rejected on all affected tables
- [ ] Verify a user cannot read or modify another user's `custom_tasks`, `planting_task_events`, or `plants`
- [ ] Check for any `bed_photos` / `planting_photos` rows with `user_id = NULL` that may now be inaccessible â€” backfill if needed

EOF
\)")
